meta.source: CATALOG_05 Instruction Manual (TC1601T05-8, Dec 2024 Ver 8.0) + assembly notes from CATALOG_01/02/03
meta.scope: Installation, replacement, torque specs, liner/inner-tube length tables, safety warnings for TOKINARC MIG/MAG robotic torches
meta.purpose: Source data for fine-tune QA dataset — Installation QA + Troubleshooting QA. NOT a runtime catalog.
meta.note: Extracted from technical drawings and procedure pages. Values are manufacturer internal standards, not guaranteed specs.

fixes: Added 8 Wire-Clamp inner tube variants (Cat05 p.5)
Added inner-tube conditional offset notes for liner protrusion (Cat05 p.6)
Added catalog-style aliases to liner protrusion table
Added wire feeder model reference YWE-WFR42DX2 (Cat05 p.5)
Renamed 4 WX part_role values to match PartCategory enum (NozzleNut→WXNozzleNut, etc.)
Linked WX-role steps to actual data parts (061445/061448/061449)
Extended coverage to 10 robotic torches via inheritance (TK-308RW/ALW, DSRC-3531, TR-300R/308R, YMENS-300R/308R/500R/508R/250RA)
Added 10 inner-tube entries and 9 protrusion entries via inheritance
Added coverage_inheritance mapping table for cross-system routing

file_structure.note: Single file maintained for atomic updates and simpler cross-referencing with data/schema/matrix.

fixes: Added adapter_fittings section with 15 entries from Cat05 p.26 (audit Section 9 GAP)
Enriched 7 adapters with sub_parts field for 2-way lookup
Added rep_tip, rep_nozzle, rep_insulator, rep_orifice procedures
Added ts_spatter_excessive, ts_gas_leak_extended, ts_wire_stuck, ts_arc_unstable, ts_weld_quality_poor troubleshooting
All new entries include related_parts for eval code_not_mentioned fix

warnings.context: Reassembling insulation spacer (YMSA-500W/508W and related water-cooled torches)
warnings.text: Always apply silicone coating when assembling the insulation spacer. Failure to do so may cause a ground fault.

warnings.context: Tightening tip adapter to torch body
warnings.text: Over-tightening the tip adapter damages the torch body. Tighten to 8 N·m maximum.
Over-tightening the tip damages both the tip and the tip adapter. Tighten to 3 N·m. Use a tool with a preset torque value.

warnings.context: Tightening tip body on WX water-cooled torches
warnings.text: Over-tightening the tip body damages the WX center ceramic. Be careful while fastening.
The O-ring at the end of the liner prevents gas leaks. Do not omit it when reinstalling the liner.
Remove burr after cutting the liner. Burr obstructs wire feeding.
Coating O-rings with molybdenum reduces the risk of damage when attaching or detaching parts. O-rings are commercially available — replace periodically.
Connect the welding cable as close as possible to the base metal and secure it tightly. A loose connection or incomplete contact causes heat buildup and fire risk.
The nozzle and contact tip become very hot after use and can cause serious burns. Do not touch immediately after welding.
WX orifices (034120 air-cooled, 034121 water-cooled) are NOT interchangeable with standard N Orifice S (003002) — different geometry and coolant routing.

warnings.context: Welding wire, contact tip, and tip body are CHARGED while torch is operating
warnings.text: DANGER: Never touch charged parts (welding wire, contact tip, tip body) while the welding torch is turned ON. Direct contact with charged parts can cause fatal electric shocks or burns. Turn OFF all input power supplies at the distribution box before any inspection or maintenance.

warnings.context: Welding equipment generates a magnetic field while ON
warnings.text: WARNING: Welding equipment generates a magnetic field that may affect sensors, gauges, and heartbeat pacemakers. Personnel with a pacemaker must not approach operating welding equipment or enter workshops where welding equipment is being used without prior medical approval.

warnings.context: Welding in confined spaces reduces oxygen content
warnings.text: DANGER: Welding in confined spaces reduces the oxygen content in the air and can result in suffocation. Always use proper ventilation equipment to vent gases locally or entirely as stipulated by safety regulations. When welding in a confined space, wear a breathing apparatus and work only under the supervision of a properly trained supervisor.

warnings.context: Welding plated or treated metals generates toxic fumes
warnings.text: CAUTION: Welding metal plated with zinc generates toxic fumes. Remove the plating before welding or wear adequate absorption equipment. Welding near degreasing, cleaning, or demisting operations may also generate toxic gases — avoid welding in such areas.

DANGER: Do not weld gas cylinders that still contain gas. Do not weld sealed tanks or pipes — rupture and explosion can occur due to internal pressure buildup from welding heat. Explosions can also occur if an arc is generated near containers of flammable substances such as gasoline.

CAUTION: Do not use the welding torch where flying spatter can ignite flammable materials. Keep base metals away from flammable materials immediately after welding — they may still be hot. Remove flammable materials on the OTHER side of ceilings, floors, and walls being welded — sparks can travel through and ignite them. Keep a fire extinguisher close to the welding area.

troubleshooting.symptom: Wire feeding is not smooth / unstable
likely_causes: Liner clogged with wire shavings, rust, or dirt
troubleshooting.recommended_action: Replace the liner (rep_liner). If problem persists, replace the inner tube (rep_inner_tube).

likely_causes: Silicone coating not applied on insulation spacer
troubleshooting.recommended_action: Disassemble and apply silicone coating on the insulation spacer, then reassemble.

troubleshooting.symptom: Gas leak at the liner connection
troubleshooting.recommended_action: Check and replace the liner O-ring (036035).
Replace torch body. Use a torque wrench set to 8 N·m for future installations.

troubleshooting.symptom: WX center ceramic cracked or damaged
troubleshooting.recommended_action: Replace the WX center ceramic. Tighten the tip body carefully during reassembly.

likely_causes: Contact tip hole worn beyond spec
Wire feeding speed or voltage parameters incorrect
troubleshooting.recommended_action: Clean or replace nozzle. Replace worn contact tip. Check orifice gas holes. Verify welding parameters.

troubleshooting.symptom: Gas leak from torch / rò khí / khí không ra đủ
troubleshooting.recommended_action: Check liner O-ring (036035). Inspect orifice and insulator. Reinstall nozzle properly.

troubleshooting.symptom: Wire stuck / kẹt dây / dây hàn bị kẹt không chạy
likely_causes: Liner clogged with wire shavings or rust
troubleshooting.recommended_action: Replace liner. Check inner tube condition. Verify liner length per model specification.

troubleshooting.symptom: Hồ quang không ổn định / arc unstable / hồ quang nhảy loạn
likely_causes: Contact tip hole worn or oversized
troubleshooting.recommended_action: Replace contact tip. Check orifice. Inspect insulator for cracks.

troubleshooting.symptom: Mối hàn xấu / poor weld quality / mối hàn rỗ khí
likely_causes: Inadequate shielding gas due to blocked orifice or nozzle
Contact tip worn causing arc instability
Liner feeding issues causing wire speed variation
troubleshooting.recommended_action: Inspect and replace nozzle/orifice. Replace worn contact tip. Check liner condition.

torque_specs.tool_recommended: Handy Tip Changer 046703 with TG3 torque grip (made by TONE)
torque_specs.warning: Over-tightening damages the tip and tip adapter. Use a tool with a preset torque value.

applies_to: tip adapter to torch body connection
torque_specs.warning: CRITICAL: over-tightening damages the torch body. Tighten carefully.

applies_to: tip body replacement on all robotic torch models
torque_specs.warning: When tip body is damaged by heat or screw is jammed, replace carefully. Over-tightening can damage threads.

torque_specs.tool_recommended: 046703 — compatible with all 6.35mm (1/4 inch) torque drivers
applies_to: nozzles with inner diameter 12mm or wider
torque_specs.warning: Tips are installed with proper torque every time; tip replacement possible without removing the nozzle.

inner_tube_length_table.note: DSRC Liner 1.1m. Available in 1.1/1.2/1.3/1.4/1.5/1.6/1.8/2.1/2.5m lengths (Cat03 p.13).

liner_protrusion_table.note: Inherits from TK-308RR — Water-cooled variant of TK-308RR (Cat05 p.13). Same inner tube + teaching.
Inherits from TK-308RR — Water-cooled aluminum variant (Cat05 p.14). Same MH mounting as TK-308RR.
Inherits from YMXA-300R — TR-sensor variant — same body as YMXA-300R but with TR shock sensor (Cat05 p.4).
Inherits from YMXA-308R — TR-sensor variant — same body as YMXA-308R but with TR shock sensor (Cat05 p.4).
Inherits from YMSA-300R — MOTOMAN-EA variant of YMSA-300R (Cat03 p.10, Cat05 p.23). For AR700/AR900/AR1440E.
Inherits from YMSA-308R — MOTOMAN-EA variant of YMSA-308R (Cat03 p.10, Cat05 p.23).
Inherits from YMSA-500R — MOTOMAN-EA variant of YMSA-500R (Cat03 p.10, Cat05 p.24).
Inherits from YMSA-508R — MOTOMAN-EA variant of YMSA-508R (Cat03 p.10, Cat05 p.24).
Inherits from YMSA-250RA — MOTOMAN-EA variant of YMSA-250RA for aluminum (Cat03 p.10, Cat05 p.25).

liner_protrusion_inner_tube_offset.note: Cat05 p.6: YMXA series ships 'Inner Tube less' as standard. If user installs the optional inner tube, ADD 35 mm to the protrusion length L listed in liner_protrusion_table.

Cat05 p.6: YMSA standard models ship 'Inner Tube less' as standard. If user installs the optional inner tube, ADD 125 mm to the protrusion length L listed in liner_protrusion_table. Wire-Clamp variants and TK/ACC/SRCT families are NOT affected.

assembly_sequences.name: NEWβ/WX Air-Cooled Nozzle assembly

steps.action: Set end parts into the tip adapter and tighten the tip
steps.note: Confirm tip inner bore matches the wire diameter

steps.action: Insert the orifice into the tip adapter
Screw the nozzle in until it does not loosen
Place a wrench on the milled face of the nozzle spacer and tighten firmly

assembly_sequences.warning: If the tip body is over-tightened, the center ceramic will be damaged.

assembly_sequences.name: WX Water-Cooled Nozzle assembly

steps.action: Insert the WX water-cooled nozzle orifice into the tip adapter
For YMSA-500AW: insert the water-cooled nozzle sleeve
Insert the water-cooled nozzle firmly into the milled face of the nozzle spacer
Tighten the nozzle set nut using a wrench on both milled faces

assembly_sequences.warning: Do not damage the water-cooled nozzle side when rotating the wrenches. Cooling water flows through the nozzle end, reducing spatter buildup and improving contact tip durability.

assembly_sequences.name: WX Nozzle Spacer / Center Ceramic positioning

steps.action: Insert the nozzle spacer firmly and completely into the milled face of the torch body
Tighten the WX center ceramic and nozzle spacer together

assembly_sequences.warning: Be careful not to damage the center ceramic while fastening. Over-tightening the tip body damages the center ceramic.
assembly_sequences.oring_note: Torch body uses O-rings in two places — JASO 1009 and JASO 1015. Nozzle spacer uses JASO 1015 and JASO 1016. Coating O-rings with molybdenum reduces damage risk when attaching/detaching.

assembly_sequences.name: Torch-to-wire-feeder connection

steps.action: Connect power cable to wire feeder (YWE-WFR42DX2)
Attach gas hose with 9/16-18UNF fitting
Secure adapter φ20 connection

assembly_sequences.warning: Welding cable should be connected as close as possible to the base metal and secured tightly. Loose connection causes heat-related fire risk.

wire_feeder.note: Standard wire feeder for YMXA/YMSA torch series. See Cat05 p.5 [3] for full dimensions.

replacement_procedures.name: Replace Tip Body
replacement_procedures.trigger: Tip body damaged by heat, or screw jammed

steps.action: Remove the damaged tip body
Clean off any spatter adhered to threads
Install new tip body and tighten to 8 N·m

cautions: Tighten carefully — over-tightening damages threads

replacement_procedures.name: Replace Inner Tube
replacement_procedures.trigger: Inner tube is worn, wire feeding becomes unstable

steps.action: Identify the correct inner tube part number for the torch model (see inner_tube_length_table)
Remove the worn inner tube
Insert the new inner tube of matching length

cautions: Inner tube length is model-specific — using wrong length affects wire feeding

replacement_procedures.name: Replace Liner
replacement_procedures.trigger: Wire feeding is not smooth; liner clogged with wire shavings, rust, or dirt
torch_context: all robotic torch models with power cable

steps.action: Remove power cable from wire feeder
Unscrew the bolt and pull out the old liner
Insert the new liner fully into the power cable
Measure the length 'L' protruding from the end
Pull liner out, cut off length 'L' at the front, remove burr
Insert the adjusted liner into power cable, screw the bolt

cautions: O-ring at the end of the liner prevents gas leaks — do not omit
Cut length 'L' is model-specific — see liner_protrusion_table
Remove burr after cutting to avoid wire feeding obstruction

replacement_procedures.name: Replace YMSA-500W/508W Torch Body Tip Adapter
replacement_procedures.trigger: Tip adapter worn or damaged on water-cooled torch

steps.action: Remove the nozzle
Remove the orifice
Remove the tip using a 12mm wrench (do not loosen the tip adapter)
Remove the tip adapter using a 12mm wrench
Clean off any spatter adhered to the threads
Tighten new tip adapter to 8 N·m using a torque wrench
Tighten the tip to 3 N·m
Attach the orifice (remove spatter from threads first)
Attach the nozzle (remove spatter from threads first)

cautions: Over-tightening the tip adapter damages the torch body
Over-tightening the tip damages the tip and tip adapter
Apply silicone coating on the insulation spacer during reassembly — see warning insulation_spacer_silicone

replacement_procedures.name: Adjust Liner Length of Power Cable
replacement_procedures.trigger: After liner replacement, length must be tuned to torch model

steps.action: Look up target liner length 'L' for the specific torch model (see liner_protrusion_table)
Adjust liner so protrusion matches table value

cautions: TK/ACC/SRCT series use L=0 (no protrusion). YMXA/YMSA series have model-specific L values

replacement_procedures.name: Replace Contact Tip (Béc Hàn)
replacement_procedures.trigger: Contact tip worn, hole enlarged beyond 1.5x wire diameter, or arc unstable

steps.action: Allow torch to cool completely before handling
steps.warning: Tip is very hot after welding — risk of burns

steps.action: Remove the nozzle by pulling straight out or unscrewing
Unscrew the worn contact tip using Handy Tip Changer 046703 or wrench
Inspect orifice and insulator for spatter buildup — clean if needed
Install new contact tip matching wire diameter. Tighten to 3 N·m
Reinstall nozzle

tools: Handy Tip Changer 046703 + TG3 torque grip
cautions: Match tip hole size to wire diameter: hole = wire + 0.1-0.2mm
Over-tightening damages tip and tip adapter — use torque tool
Use CuCrZr tips (002001-002017) for CO2/MAG; Cu tips (002019) for aluminum MIG

replacement_procedures.name: Replace Nozzle / Chụp Khí
replacement_procedures.trigger: Nozzle spatter buildup excessive, nozzle deformed, or gas coverage poor

steps.action: Allow torch to cool before handling
Pull nozzle straight off torch (press-fit type N) or unscrew (threaded type)
Clean spatter from orifice and insulator threads
Install new nozzle — press firmly for press-fit, or screw until snug
Verify nozzle inner diameter clears tip — no contact between nozzle and tip

cautions: N-type nozzles are press-fit (33203, 001002 series) — do not overtighten
Check orifice is seated correctly after nozzle change
Rocket tip requires nozzle 001016 only; Long tip requires 001004 or 038042

replacement_procedures.name: Replace Insulator / Cách Điện
replacement_procedures.trigger: Insulator cracked, burned, or causing electrical shorts to nozzle
torch_context: all N and D robotic torch models

steps.action: Power OFF welding system completely before proceeding
steps.warning: Tip body is electrically charged when system is ON

steps.action: Remove nozzle
Remove tip
Remove orifice
Slide out old insulator
Install new insulator — ensure correct size (S=350A: 004002, L=500A: 004001)
Reinstall orifice, tip (3 N·m), nozzle in reverse order

cautions: N S insulator (004002, 39mm) for 350A torches; N L insulator (004001, 38mm) for 500A torches
D insulator (023015) different from N insulator — not interchangeable
Incorrect insulator size causes gas flow obstruction or arc instability

replacement_procedures.name: Replace Orifice / Sứ Chia Khí
replacement_procedures.trigger: Orifice cracked, spatter-blocked, or gas flow uneven
torch_context: all N and D robotic torch models

steps.action: Remove nozzle
Remove tip
Lift out old orifice — it sits between tip body and insulator
Install new orifice matching torch series (003002 for N 350A, 003001 for N 500A)
Reinstall tip and nozzle

cautions: N Orifice S (003002, 21mm) for 350A; N Orifice L (003001, 26mm) for 500A — NOT interchangeable
D Orifice (023014) for Daihen/OTC — different from N orifice
WX orifice (034120/034121) unique to WX water-cooled torch

power_cable_length_spec.note: Please specify cable length (m) according to robot model. Available in 0.1m increments.

adapters.name: 308RR-T Adapter
adapters.note: For former standard type. Includes T adapter fitting + wire guide pin.

adapters.name: 308RR-N Adapter
adapters.note: N adapter fitting + N/B/H guide pin + N adapter gas hose.

adapters.name: 308RR-D Adapter
adapters.note: For DAIHEN type. D adapter fitting.

adapters.name: 308RR-B Adapter
adapters.note: B adapter fitting + B adapter gas hose.

adapters.name: 308RR-H Adapter
adapters.note: H adapter fitting + H adapter gas hose.

adapters.name: 308RR-N-D Adapter
adapters.note: N→D converter adapter. Includes N-D Adapter Fitting (021013) + DSRC-3531 P/C Attachment (22202A).

adapters.name: 308RR-N-DD Adapter
adapters.note: N→DD (DAIHEN CMRE-741) converter adapter. Includes N-D Adapter Fitting (021013) + DSRC-3531 P/C Attachment (22202A) + CMRE741 Guide Adapter (023024).

torch_mounting_brackets.name: YMXA Bracket Assembly
torch_mounting_brackets.note: Type MA — with internally mounted power cable. Requires YMA Flange (YJ1305277).

torch_mounting_brackets.name: YMA Flange
torch_mounting_brackets.note: Type MA flange. Use with YMXA Bracket Assembly.

torch_mounting_brackets.name: YMH Bracket Assembly
torch_mounting_brackets.note: Type MH — with externally mounted power cable. Requires YMH Flange (YJ1307086). For TK-308RR.

torch_mounting_brackets.name: YMSH Bracket Assembly
torch_mounting_brackets.note: Type MH bracket for SRCT-308R with shock sensor. Requires YMH Flange (YJ1307086).

torch_mounting_brackets.name: YMH Flange
torch_mounting_brackets.note: Type MH flange. Use with YMH or YMSH Bracket Assembly.

coverage_inheritance.purpose: Maps torches that inherit assembly procedures from a parent model

TK-308RW.note: Water-cooled variant of TK-308RR (Cat05 p.13). Same inner tube + teaching.

TK-308ALW.note: Water-cooled aluminum variant (Cat05 p.14). Same MH mounting as TK-308RR.

DSRC-3531.note: DSRC-3531 D-type with built-in sensor — see Cat05 p.24 parts drawing. Liner: 023531 (1.1m) or 023539 (2.5m).

TR-300R.note: TR-sensor variant — same body as YMXA-300R but with TR shock sensor (Cat05 p.4).

TR-308R.note: TR-sensor variant — same body as YMXA-308R but with TR shock sensor (Cat05 p.4).

YMENS-300R.note: MOTOMAN-EA variant of YMSA-300R (Cat03 p.10, Cat05 p.23). For AR700/AR900/AR1440E.

YMENS-308R.note: MOTOMAN-EA variant of YMSA-308R (Cat03 p.10, Cat05 p.23).

YMENS-500R.note: MOTOMAN-EA variant of YMSA-500R (Cat03 p.10, Cat05 p.24).

YMENS-508R.note: MOTOMAN-EA variant of YMSA-508R (Cat03 p.10, Cat05 p.24).

YMENS-250RA.note: MOTOMAN-EA variant of YMSA-250RA for aluminum (Cat03 p.10, Cat05 p.25).

coverage_inheritance.note: Several robotic torches share identical assembly procedures with their parent model (e.g. TK-308RW inherits from TK-308RR, YMENS-300R from YMSA-300R). This table lets the bot answer 'replace inner tube on YMENS-300R' by routing to YMSA-300R procedures.

adapter_fittings.name: DSRC-3531 P/C Attachment
adapter_fittings.note: Special — không thuộc adapter chính. Dùng riêng cho DSRC-3531.
